terms.definition: The useful substances present in food that our body needs to grow, get energy, and stay healthy. The main nutrients are carbohydrates, proteins, fats, vitamins, and minerals.
terms.exampleUsage: Fruits and vegetables are full of important nutrients like vitamins and minerals.

terms.definition: Nutrients found in foods like rice, roti, bread, and potatoes that give our body energy to work, play, and study.
terms.exampleUsage: Rice and roti are rich in carbohydrates that give us energy for the whole day.

terms.definition: Nutrients found in foods like dal, milk, paneer, eggs, and fish that help our body grow and repair itself.
terms.exampleUsage: Children need proteins from dal and milk to grow tall and strong.

terms.definition: Nutrients found in ghee, oil, butter, and nuts that give our body extra energy and keep us warm.
terms.exampleUsage: A little ghee on your roti gives you fats for extra energy.

terms.definition: Nutrients found in fruits and vegetables that protect our body from diseases and help different body parts work properly.
terms.exampleUsage: Eating an orange gives us Vitamin C that helps us fight colds.

terms.definition: Nutrients like iron, calcium, and iodine found in food that help build strong bones and teeth and keep our blood healthy.
terms.exampleUsage: Milk has calcium, a mineral that makes our bones and teeth strong.

terms.definition: A diet that includes the right amounts of all food groups: carbohydrates, proteins, fats, vitamins, minerals, and water.
terms.exampleUsage: An Indian thali with roti, dal, sabzi, rice, and curd is a balanced diet.

terms.definition: The process by which our body breaks down the food we eat into very tiny pieces so it can be used by the body for energy, growth, and repair.
terms.exampleUsage: Digestion starts in the mouth when we chew our food properly.

terms.definition: The fibre found in plant foods like whole grains, fruits, and vegetables that helps food move through our digestive system. Also called dietary fibre.
terms.exampleUsage: Eating whole wheat roti gives us roughage that keeps our stomach healthy.

terms.definition: The watery liquid in our mouth that helps make food soft and slippery and begins the process of digestion.
terms.exampleUsage: When we chew food, saliva mixes with it and starts breaking it down.

terms.definition: A health problem caused by not eating enough nutritious food or eating too much of the wrong food. It can make a person weak and sick.
terms.exampleUsage: Children who do not eat enough fruits and vegetables can suffer from malnutrition.

terms.definition: Methods used to keep food fresh and safe for a longer time, such as drying, pickling, refrigerating, or adding salt and sugar.
terms.exampleUsage: Making mango pickle is a form of food preservation that keeps mangoes eatable for months.
